title: tolmachevia integrifolia subsp. neomexicana
intro: Tolmachevia integrifolia subsp. neomexicana, also known as the New Mexico golden ragwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family. This plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, making it a potential treatment for wounds and infections. The health benefits of Tolmachevia integrifolia subsp. neomexicana include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair, as well as its potential to act as an antimicrobial agent. Tolmachevia integrifolia subsp. neomexicana can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used in traditional medicine.
